source.domain: rail, transit, elevator/escalator maintenance, public-infrastructure service tools, and alignment fixtures

requirements.part_name: Rail Clip Tension Verification Gauge - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Rail Clip Tension Verification Gauge'. The broader
use case is: Railway track maintenance crews checking elastic rail fastening clips during inspection
rounds. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Stepped bridge gauge body with
rail-foot locating pads, clip clearance slot, and probe reference boss. All other product elements
are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role:
Slides over a rail clip and provides a mechanical reference surface for measuring whether clip
deflection remains within service limits. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Requires nested cutouts, asymmetric stepped surfaces, datum pads, rail-profile clearances,
and functional gauge features that must relate precisely to one another. The part is made from low-
carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate
blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that
same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded
pieces.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 230 mm x 135 mm x 5 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 11 mm on the length centerline at X=27 mm and X=203 mm, Y=67 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 76 mm long x 14 mm wide through the part, centered at X=115 mm, Y=67 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 57 mm x 45 mm x 1 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: serrated contact edge
functional_features.description: Cut 9 equal triangular serrations across the rear edge, each 5 mm deep.

functional_features.name: integral hook lip
functional_features.description: Leave an integral hook lip on one short end, projecting 16 mm and undercut 5 mm for registration.

tolerances: Overall envelope dimensions: +/-0.20 mm.
Hole diameters and slot widths: +/-0.10 mm.
Hole and slot center positions from datums B and C: +/-0.15 mm.
Pocket depths and relief depths: +/-0.10 mm.
Nonfunctional chamfers and radii: +/-0.30 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.
The feature named 'single rectangular metal body' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'mounting hole pattern' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'functional center feature' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'top relief pocket' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'edge chamfers' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'serrated contact edge' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'integral hook lip' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Flat datums remain planar and usable; pockets or slots do not accidentally break unsupported walls.
negative_requirements: Represent exactly one metal part only.
Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.
Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.
Do not replace through holes, slots, pockets, grooves, flats, tapers, or chamfers with cosmetic markings.
Do not omit datum-critical features just because they are small.
Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'Rail Clip Tension Verification Gauge'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.
requirements.immutable_requirement_note: After review, this English requirement is the product truth for later SubCAD code generation. Do not simplify it to fit current SubCAD limitations.

source.domain: rail, transit, elevator/escalator maintenance, public-infrastructure service tools, and alignment fixtures

requirements.part_name: Transit Door Roller Alignment Jig - single metal part

material.reason: strong cylindrical metal part with threaded and slotted details

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Transit Door Roller Alignment Jig'. The broader use
case is: Metro and light-rail maintenance depots servicing sliding passenger door mechanisms. The
chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Machined clamp block with V-track seat, offset
roller cradle, locking screw bosses, and calibration faces. All other product elements are external
reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Temporarily
mounts to the door track and positions a roller carriage at the correct height and lateral offset
during replacement. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Combines track-
following geometry, offset reference planes, cylindrical roller nests, clamp screw clearances, and
inspection datums. The part is made from 4140 alloy steel, prehard using round bar stock. Start from
one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill
secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the
deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 57 mm and length 75 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 14 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 24 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 19 mm wide over 67 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M6 holes through the top flat at X=25 mm and X=50 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 4 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.
acceptance_checklist: The output contains one continuous metal solid representing one part.
The stock family, material intent, envelope dimensions, and datum orientation match this requirement.
The feature named 'single cylindrical body' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'axial through bore' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'end counterbores' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'milled reference flat' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'radial clamp holes' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'split relief slit' is present with the stated size and position.
The feature named 'outside edge treatment' is present with the stated size and position.
All required chamfers, reliefs, and edge treatments are visible in STEP and STL outputs.
No extra parts, fasteners, hardware, labels, cosmetic meshes, or nonmetal components are present.
Round features are coaxial unless the requirement explicitly says they are radial or offset.
negative_requirements: Represent exactly one metal part only.
Do not model screws, nuts, springs, bushings, bearings, rubber pads, plastic covers, electronics, labels, handles, knobs, adhesives, weld beads, or separate inserts.
Do not convert the part into an assembly or multiple bodies.
Do not replace through holes, slots, pockets, grooves, flats, tapers, or chamfers with cosmetic markings.
Do not omit datum-critical features just because they are small.
Do not broaden the requirement back into the full product idea named 'Transit Door Roller Alignment Jig'; this requirement is only for its chosen metal part.

source.raw_idea_title: Escalator Step Chain Pitch Wear Gauge

requirements.part_name: Escalator Step Chain Pitch Wear Gauge - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the cylinder axis as X. The left faced end is datum A at X=0. The radial direction through the largest flat or slot is datum B. Positive Z points upward from the part centerline when the main flat faces up.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Escalator Step Chain Pitch Wear Gauge'. The broader
use case is: Escalator maintenance teams checking step chain elongation without removing the full
chain assembly. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Long gauge bar with
precision pin pockets, tapered lead-in slots, relief channels, and engraved limit reference zones.
All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must
perform this mechanical role: Indexes across several chain pins and shows whether accumulated pitch
wear exceeds the allowed limit. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Tests repeated feature spacing, elongated slots, tolerance-critical pocket placement, thin ribs, and
long-part proportional accuracy. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent
using round bar stock. Start from one cut length of round metal bar. Turn the outside, face both
ends, bore the center, then mill secondary flats, slots, and radial holes as needed. No separate
inserts or fasteners are part of the deliverable.

functional_features.name: single cylindrical body
functional_features.description: Turn one coaxial cylinder to OD 45 mm and length 85 mm from solid round bar.

functional_features.name: axial through bore
functional_features.description: Machine a centered through bore diameter 9 mm along the full length; both bore mouths have 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers.

functional_features.name: end counterbores
functional_features.description: Add shallow concentric counterbores on both ends, diameter 19 mm x 3 mm deep.

functional_features.name: milled reference flat
functional_features.description: Mill one longitudinal flat 15 mm wide over 77 mm of length, centered on the top side.

functional_features.name: radial clamp holes
functional_features.description: Add two radial tapped M5 holes through the top flat at X=28 mm and X=56 mm.

functional_features.name: split relief slit
functional_features.description: Cut one full-length radial slit 2 mm wide from the outside to the axial bore on datum-B side.

functional_features.name: outside edge treatment
functional_features.description: Break all outside circular edges with 1.0 mm chamfers and keep internal bore edges visibly chamfered.

functional_features.name: cross relief slot
functional_features.description: Machine one transverse slot 4 mm wide across the top flat at X=42 mm, depth 4 mm.

tolerances: Outside diameter and axial bore diameter: +/-0.05 mm.
Concentricity of bore to outer diameter: within 0.05 mm TIR.
Milled flats and slots: +/-0.15 mm unless otherwise specified.
Nonfunctional chamfers: +/-0.3 mm.

requirements.part_name: Elevator Guide Rail Fishplate Drilling Template - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Elevator Guide Rail Fishplate Drilling Template'.
The broader use case is: Elevator installation and modernization crews replacing or adding guide
rail splice plates. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Profiled rail saddle
template with drill bush pockets, side clamp ears, and relieved central web clearance. All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Locates on the elevator guide rail profile and guides drilling for accurately
positioned fishplate bolt holes. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because:
Requires a recognizable rail-profile negative, multiple coaxial hole features, bushing counterbores,
clamp tabs, and nonuniform wall thickness. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or
equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside
profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are
called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 60 mm long x 18 mm wide through the part, centered at X=90 mm, Y=72 mm.

functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 45 mm x 48 mm x 2 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.description: Add a central counterbore diameter 26 mm x 3 mm deep around the center feature.

source.raw_idea_title: Platform Screen Door Threshold Height Gauge

requirements.part_name: Platform Screen Door Threshold Height Gauge - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Platform Screen Door Threshold Height Gauge'. The
broader use case is: Transit station service teams verifying the height relationship between
platform coping, train floor, and platform screen door threshold. The chosen deliverable is only the
metal body implied by: Folded-profile gauge block with wide base foot, raised sill contact ledge,
stepped limit faces, and handle slot. All other product elements are external reference items and
must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical role: Spans from platform datum surfaces
to the screen door sill and provides fixed reference steps for allowable vertical offsets. It is
intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Exercises stepped datum geometry, large
flat contact faces, ergonomic through-slots, chamfered edges, and clearly separated measurement
levels. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular block
stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then
machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid
piece.

functional_features.name: functional center feature

functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 25 mm x 23 mm x 15 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

adjustment. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests tapered solids,
curved contact faces, engraved reference bands, through-holes normal to sloped surfaces, and robust
end-stop geometry. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using rectangular
block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides,
then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same

source.raw_idea_title: Escalator Comb Plate Tooth Inspection Fixture

requirements.part_name: Escalator Comb Plate Tooth Inspection Fixture - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Escalator Comb Plate Tooth Inspection Fixture'. The
broader use case is: Escalator service technicians checking comb plate tooth spacing, straightness,
and broken-tooth replacement fit. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Comb-
matching gauge plate with repeated narrow slots, front datum lip, offset handle boss, and clearance
scallops. All other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part
must perform this mechanical role: Indexes against multiple comb teeth and provides reference slots
for evaluating pitch and tooth alignment. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Requires dense repeated thin features, pitch consistency, shallow datum lips, local
reliefs, and a mix of plate and raised boss geometry. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM
A36 or equivalent using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the
outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If
bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 180 mm x 85 mm x 10 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 8 mm on the length centerline at X=17 mm and X=163 mm, Y=42 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 60 mm long x 15 mm wide through the part, centered at X=90 mm, Y=42 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket

requirements.part_name: Catenary Bracket Angle Setting Fixture - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Catenary Bracket Angle Setting Fixture'. The
broader use case is: Overhead electrification crews aligning support brackets for tram, metro, or
rail catenary hardware. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Angular fixture
block with bolt slots, inclined reference plane, gusset-like ribs, and locating dowel holes. All
other product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform
this mechanical role: Temporarily bolts to a mast bracket and presents angled reference faces for
setting the cantilever arm orientation. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus
because: Combines angled planes, slotted fastener holes, ribbed reinforcement, datum hole patterns,
and multi-axis feature relationships. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent
using sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile,
machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called
out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 9 mm on the length centerline at X=12 mm and X=208 mm, Y=30 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 73 mm long x 13 mm wide through the part, centered at X=110 mm, Y=30 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 55 mm x 20 mm x 5 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: machined angled reference face
functional_features.description: Machine one top reference face at 11 degrees over the last 44 mm of length.

source.raw_idea_title: Manhole Cover Lifter Hook Wear Gauge

requirements.part_name: Manhole Cover Lifter Hook Wear Gauge - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Manhole Cover Lifter Hook Wear Gauge'. The broader
use case is: Municipal infrastructure maintenance crews inspecting lifting hooks used for heavy road
and utility covers. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: Thick go/no-go gauge
plate with hook-profile cutouts, stepped throat references, grip slot, and hanging hole. All other
product elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this
mechanical role: Checks hook throat opening, tip wear, and shank deformation against fixed go/no-go
profiles. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Tests complex internal
profiles, multiple functional cutouts, thick plate modeling, rounded stress-relief corners, and
labeled reference geometry. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using
sheet or plate stock. Start from one flat sheet or plate blank. Cut the outside profile, machine
holes, slots, pockets, lips, and relief features into that same piece. If bends are called out, they
are bends in the same sheet part, not separate welded pieces.

functional_features.name: single rectangular metal body
functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 115 mm x 85 mm x 9 mm.

functional_features.name: mounting hole pattern
functional_features.description: Drill two through holes diameter 5 mm on the length centerline at X=17 mm and X=98 mm, Y=42 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 38 mm long x 8 mm wide through the part, centered at X=57 mm, Y=42 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 28 mm x 28 mm x 3 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: serrated contact edge
functional_features.description: Cut 10 equal triangular serrations across the rear edge, each 5 mm deep.

functional_features.name: integral hook lip
functional_features.description: Leave an integral hook lip on one short end, projecting 9 mm and undercut 2 mm for registration.

requirements.part_name: Elevator Brake Pad Centering Fixture - single metal part

material.material: low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent

requirements.manufacturing_context: Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into that same solid piece.
requirements.datum_orientation: Use the finished bottom face as datum A. Use the long left edge as datum B and the near short edge as datum C. The origin is the lower-left-near corner of the finished rectangular envelope; X follows length, Y follows width, and Z is upward.
requirements.full_description:
Make the single metal part for the product idea 'Elevator Brake Pad Centering Fixture'. The broader
use case is: Elevator machine-room maintenance crews replacing or adjusting brake pads on traction
machines. The chosen deliverable is only the metal body implied by: C-shaped centering fixture with
curved drum reference face, pad pocket, spacer lands, and thumb-screw bosses. All other product
elements are external reference items and must not be modeled. The part must perform this mechanical
role: Centers the brake pad relative to the drum or disc face while maintaining a defined service
clearance. It is intentionally included in the SubCAD limit corpus because: Requires curved
reference geometry, open C-frame topology, pocketed locating surfaces, opposing clearance lands, and
threaded-boss placement. The part is made from low-carbon steel, ASTM A36 or equivalent using
rectangular block stock. Start from one rectangular metal block or plate. Saw oversize, face all
datum sides, then machine the pockets, holes, slots, angled faces, grooves, and edge treatments into
that same solid piece.

functional_features.description: Machine one body with finished envelope 165 mm x 110 mm x 55 mm.

functional_features.name: functional center feature
functional_features.description: Machine a central obround slot 55 mm long x 18 mm wide through the part, centered at X=82 mm, Y=55 mm.

functional_features.name: top relief pocket
functional_features.description: Mill a rectangular relief pocket 41 mm x 36 mm x 7 mm deep on the top face, centered between the mounting holes.

functional_features.name: edge chamfers
functional_features.description: Apply 1.0 mm x 45 degree chamfers to all top outside edges and 0.5 mm chamfers to hole and slot mouths.

functional_features.name: side tapped hole
functional_features.description: Tap one side hole M9 from the right long edge into the central pocket; hole axis is parallel to Y.
